task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully logged into the Rancher interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher dashboard is visible in a web browser, indicating a successful login (e.g., URL shows localhost, user profile or cluster dashboard is displayed).

task_completion.description: Verify that the PersistentVolumeClaims in the target namespace are successfully bound.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rancher UI (Storage -> PersistentVolumeClaims) or via a terminal 'kubectl get pvc -n data-pipeline' output, the PVCs show a status of 'Bound'.

task_completion.description: Verify the kafka-broker deployment has at least one Running pod.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rancher Workloads/Deployments view or terminal output, the 'kafka-broker' deployment in the 'data-pipeline' namespace shows at least 1 active/Running pod (e.g., 1/1).

task_completion.description: Verify the minio-store deployment has at least one Running pod.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rancher Workloads/Deployments view or terminal output, the 'minio-store' deployment in the 'data-pipeline' namespace shows at least 1 active/Running pod (e.g., 1/1).

task_completion.description: Verify the elastic-search deployment has at least one Running pod.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rancher Workloads/Deployments view or terminal output, the 'elastic-search' deployment in the 'data-pipeline' namespace shows at least 1 active/Running pod (e.g., 1/1).

task_completion.description: Verify the analytics-db deployment has at least one Running pod.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rancher Workloads/Deployments view or terminal output, the 'analytics-db' deployment in the 'data-pipeline' namespace shows at least 1 active/Running pod (e.g., 1/1).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually interacted with the Rancher UI or used kubectl commands to remediate the issues.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a sequence of navigating the Rancher UI, editing YAMLs, or typing kubectl commands in a terminal, rather than just a final static state appearing out of nowhere.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not bypass the storage requirement by simply removing the volume mounts from the deployments.
integrity.visual_evidence: The deployments or pods should still show attached volumes/PVCs in the UI or terminal output, confirming the storage misconfigurations were fixed rather than deleted.

integrity.description: Check that the UI or terminal outputs were not spoofed.
integrity.visual_evidence: Fonts, alignments, and UI elements in Rancher should look consistent. Terminal outputs should match standard kubectl formatting without obvious text manipulation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target namespace is explicitly 'data-pipeline'. The four target deployments that must be remediated and have at least one Running pod are: 'kafka-broker', 'minio-store', 'elastic-search', and 'analytics-db'.
